title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: A desert planet, political betrayal, ecological prophecy, and a reluctant hero navigating empire and destiny.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
description: A lone scientist wakes up in space and must solve an extinction-level scientific puzzle to save Earth.

title: The Name of the Wind
author: Patrick Rothfuss
description: A gifted young musician and magician recounts his rise from poverty to legend.

title: The Lies of Locke Lamora
author: Scott Lynch
description: A brilliant con artist leads a gang of thieves through elaborate schemes in a dangerous canal city.

title: The Martian
author: Andy Weir
description: An astronaut stranded on Mars survives through math, botany, and relentless problem-solving.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A sweeping narrative of human evolution, culture, economics, and power structures.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: Practical systems for building good habits through small consistent behavior changes.

title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
description: Strategies for focused concentration in a distracted world and producing high-value work.

title: The Psychology of Money
author: Morgan Housel
description: Stories about risk, wealth, and how behavior often matters more than technical finance knowledge.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: A landmark exploration of intuitive and deliberate thinking and how biases shape decisions.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
description: A psychotherapist investigates why a famous artist stopped speaking after a shocking crime.

title: Gone Girl
author: Gillian Flynn
description: A marriage unravels under media scrutiny when a wife disappears and suspicion falls on her husband.

title: Educated
author: Tara Westover
description: A woman raised in an isolated survivalist family pursues education and redefines her identity.

title: Becoming
author: Michelle Obama
description: An intimate journey through family, career, public service, and life in the White House.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: A comfort-loving hobbit joins a perilous quest involving dragons, treasure, and courage.

title: The Fellowship of the Ring
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: A group sets out to destroy a powerful ring before darkness consumes Middle-earth.

author: George Orwell
description: A surveillance state crushes truth, language, and individual freedom under totalitarian control.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
description: A futuristic society engineered for stability questions freedom, meaning, and human emotion.

title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: Between life and death, a woman explores alternate lives shaped by different choices.

title: A Man Called Ove
author: Fredrik Backman
description: A grumpy but deeply human widower finds unexpected community and purpose.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A shepherd follows a recurring dream and discovers meaning through a spiritual journey.

title: The Kite Runner
author: Khaled Hosseini
description: Friendship, betrayal, and redemption unfold against decades of conflict in Afghanistan.

title: Circe
author: Madeline Miller
description: A reimagining of a mythic witch's life, voice, and transformation through exile and love.

title: The Song of Achilles
author: Madeline Miller
description: An emotional retelling of Achilles and Patroclus set against the Trojan War.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: Principles and practices for writing maintainable readable code in professional teams.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
author: Martin Kleppmann
description: Core ideas for reliable scalable data systems, from storage engines to stream processing.

title: Hooked
author: Nir Eyal
description: A framework for building habit-forming products through triggers, action, and rewards.

title: The Lean Startup
author: Eric Ries
description: Build-measure-learn loops for reducing waste and validating products quickly.

title: Zero to One
author: Peter Thiel
description: A contrarian lens on innovation, monopoly, and creating new categories in technology.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt and David Thomas
description: Timeless software craftsmanship advice on thinking, tooling, and continuous improvement.
